derived_design_findings: Current v0.3 has a clean minimal decision spine but can be too eager to label EXIT_PREFERRED when risk is only moderately rising and option exit friction is neutral rather than clearly good.
Hard boundary breach and very high touch probability should create a direct HEDGE_READY path when persistence is at least MEDIUM and hedge friction is better than option exit.
A WATCH state needs a separate 'rising but not hedgeable yet' lane so drift without persistence does not become hedge intent.
KPF buffer state should remain a persistence confirmation only; sampled cases confirm using it to alter market touch probability would create false comfort.
Hedge sizing should be tied to breached/high-probability and tail acceleration, with capped ratios instead of full delta neutralization.
